channel.title: NYT > World > Middle East
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Francesca Regalado, Elian Peltier, Ephrat Livni and Farnaz Fassihi

item.title: Hours Before News of Cease-Fire, Pope Leo XIV Issues Strong Rebuke of Trump
item.description: The first U.S.-born pontiff has consistently called for dialogue to resolve the war in the Middle East. He referred to Trump’s Iran threats as ‘truly unacceptable.’
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Guglielmo Mangiapane/Reuters
item.media:description: Pope Leo XIV condemned President Trump’s threats against Iran on Tuesday.

item.title: Trump Backs Down, but Questions Remain Over Iran and the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: President Trump’s short-term intimidation may have worked, but the fundamental divides with Iran are as sharp as they were in February.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump during a White House news conference on Monday.

item.title: Iran War Timeline: Key Moments and Attacks In U.S. and Israel’s Campaign
item.description: The United States and Israel attacked Iran on Feb. 28, starting a weekslong war that spread to neighboring countries and rocked global markets.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Ahn
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Smoke rises from an explosion in Tehran on Feb. 28, the first day of the war.

item.title: Trump’s Iran Threats Look Like Self-Incrimination for Potential War Crimes
item.description: President Trump, in vowing to systematically destroy civilian infrastructure and annihilate Iran’s entire civilization, appears to be creating evidence about his intentions.
item.dc:creator: Charlie Savage
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
War Crimes, Genocide and Crimes Against Humanity
item.media:credit: Tom Brenner for The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump addressing reporters in a briefing on Monday at the White House, before his latest threat. Asked then whether he was concerned that the attacks he has threatened could amount to war crimes, Mr. Trump said: “No, I’m not.”

item.title: Russia and China Veto Proposed U.N. Resolution to Open the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The vote came hours before an 8 p.m. deadline set by President Trump to reach a deal to reopen the waterway.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Michael M. Santiago/Getty Images
item.media:description: The United Nations Security Council on Tuesday.

item.title: Democrats Condemn Trump’s Iran Threats as Some Call for His Removal Via 25th Amendment
item.description: More than 70 Democratic lawmakers, questioning his mental fitness, called for the president’s removal from office through impeachment or the 25th Amendment.
item.dc:creator: Megan Mineiro and Michael Gold
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
War Crimes, Genocide and Crimes Against Humanity
item.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times
item.media:description: Representative Joaquin Castro, Democrat of Texas, called on President Trump’s cabinet to invoke the 25th Amendment and remove him from office.

item.title: Trump Says He Has Agreed to a 2-Week Cease-Fire With Iran
item.description: President Trump announced the deal hours after threatening that a “whole civilization will die tonight” if Iran did not allow commercial vessels to transit the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: Tyler Pager, Farnaz Fassihi, Julian E. Barnes, Ronen Bergman and Michael Levenson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Iranians gathered on Tuesday for a 40th-day memorial for schoolchildren killed by a U.S. airstrike.

item.title: Some Republicans Vent Concern as Party Backs Trump’s Iran Threat
item.description: Senator Ron Johnson said he hoped President Trump was making empty threats, but most in the G.O.P. cheered his warning that Iran’s “whole civilization” would be wiped out.
item.dc:creator: Robert Jimison
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Haiyun Jiang/The New York Times
item.media:description: Senator Ron Johnson said President Trump would lose support if the U.S. military destroyed civilian infrastructure in Iran.

item.title: As Deadline Nears, Confusion Over U.S.-Iran Talks Swirls Worldwide
item.description: U.S., Iranian, Israeli and other officials offered varying accounts about the state of negotiations between Washington and Tehran.
item.dc:creator: Michael Crowley
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump set an 8 p.m. Tuesday deadline for Iran to open the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Trump’s Threat to Wipe Out a ‘Whole Civilization’ Appalls Some Conservatives
item.description: Marjorie Taylor Greene, Tucker Carlson and Senator Ron Johnson were among those pushing back against President Trump’s threats toward Iran.
item.dc:creator: Nick Corasaniti
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: Senator Ron Johnson of Wisconsin pushed back against President Trump’s threats against Iran.

item.title: Chairman of the Joint Chiefs Struggles to Justify Trump’s Threats
item.description: The president’s apocalyptic rhetoric clashes with the responsibility of Gen. Dan Caine to protect the military’s honor.
item.dc:creator: Greg Jaffe
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: General Dan Caine, the chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff, during a briefing on the war with Iran. He vowed that the force he leads would “always strike lawful targets.”

item.title: U.S. Warns of Cyberattacks Tied to Iran on Water and Energy Systems
item.description: The warning did not name specific facilities that had been struck or say whether any damage had been done.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Paul Ratje for The New York Times
item.media:description: Energy systems across the United States may be the target of cyberattacks, according to the Trump administration.

item.title: Pentagon Stays Mum on School Strike, Even as Trump Boasts of Iran Rescue
item.description: President Trump and Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth’s eagerness to recount details of the rescue of a downed airman followed weeks of silence on the deadly Tomahawk missile strike on an Iranian school.
item.dc:creator: Megan Mineiro
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump and Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth in the Oval Office on Thursday.

item.title: Iranian Officials Say Indirect Talks With U.S. Ended After Trump’s Latest Threat
item.description: As the United States’ 8 p.m. deadline to open the Strait of Hormuz approaches, Iran vowed to retaliate if its power plants were attacked.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: A group waving Iranian flags in Tehran on Tuesday.

item.title: Trump Threatens Broad Attacks in Iran. How Is Illegal Conduct in War Defined?
item.description: It is illegal for any military to target civilians, as President Trump has suggested he would in threats against Iran. But the U.S. has sought significant leeway in defining a civilian target.
item.dc:creator: Thomas Gibbons-Neff
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
War Crimes, Genocide and Crimes Against Humanity
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Airstrikes demolished Shahid Beheshti University in Tehran. The U.S. military’s law of war manual states that “the protection of civilians against the harmful effects of hostilities is one of the main purposes of the law of war.”

item.title: How Tolls in the Strait of Hormuz Would Undercut International Law
item.description: Both the U.S. and Iran are now talking about imposing fees on ships using the vital passageway. That could fray a treaty that ended centuries of lawlessness on the high seas.
item.dc:creator: Sachi Kitajima Mulkey
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Associated Press
item.media:description: A cargo ship in the Persian Gulf heading toward the Strait of Hormuz in recent weeks.

item.title: Cécile Kohler and Jacques Paris, French Couple Held in Iran, Are Free
item.description: The couple, who had been arrested during a tourist visit in 2022, were accused of spying in a case that galvanized the French public.
item.dc:creator: Catherine Porter
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: The Iranian capital on Saturday.

item.title: U.S., Israel and Iran Accelerate Strikes Ahead of Trump’s Deadline
item.description: The United States and Israel are trying to force Iran to open the Strait of Hormuz, according to American and Israel officials.
item.dc:creator: Max Bearak
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: An Iranian synagogue was destroyed from a strike in Tehran.

item.title: U.S. Strikes Kharg Island as Trump Raises Iran Threats to Apocalyptic Levels
item.description: U.S. forces launched more than 90 “restrikes” on Kharg Island, Iran’s oil export hub, early on Tuesday but have avoided hitting oil infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: Helene Cooper, Julian E. Barnes and Eric Schmitt
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Planet Labs Pbc/AFP, via Getty Images
item.media:description: Kharg Island in February before the U.S. and Israeli strikes. The United States launched a series of more than 90 strikes on the island, an Iranian oil hub, early Tuesday.

item.title: Lebanese Mourn Couple Killed in Israeli Strike, Exposing Rift Over Hezbollah
item.description: The victims had no ties to Hezbollah, and Israel said they were not targets. Many Lebanese are angry with Israel, but also with Hezbollah for embroiling their country in the war.
item.dc:creator: Hwaida Saad and Abdi Latif Dahir
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sanchez for The New York Times
item.media:description: Funerals were held in Yahchouch, Lebanon, on Tuesday.

item.title: Shelly Kittleson, American Journalist Abducted in Iraq, Is Freed
item.description: The journalist, Shelly Kittleson, was abducted by a militia allied with Iran and held for a week. Iraqi officials say she was freed in exchange for the release of militia members.
item.dc:creator: Falih Hassan, Pranav Baskar and Erika Solomon
item.media:credit: Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: Shelly Kittleson in Baghdad last month.

item.title: 6 Takeaways From the Story of Trump’s Decision to Go to War With Iran
item.description: New details from the weeks leading up to the campaign show how President Trump’s alignment with Benjamin Netanyahu and a lack of sustained opposition from his inner circle put the United States on a course to war.
item.dc:creator: Maggie Haberman and Jonathan Swan
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel and President Trump at Mar-a-Lago, his club and residence in Florida, in December.

item.title: How Trump Took the U.S. to War With Iran
item.description: In a series of Situation Room meetings, President Trump weighed his instincts against the deep concerns of his vice president and a pessimistic intelligence assessment. Here’s the inside story of how he made the fateful decision.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Swan and Maggie Haberman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Al Drago for The New York Times
item.media:description: The decision by President Trump to give the go-ahead to join Israel in attacking Iran was influenced by a presentation by Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu in February that led to a series of discussions inside the White House over the following days and weeks.

item.title: Britain Reinforces That U.S. Cannot Use British Bases for Attacks on Iran
item.description: The U.K. government underlined its previous stance that the United States could only use British bases for defensive purposes, after President Trump threatened to strike civilian targets.
item.dc:creator: Michael D. Shear
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Royal Air Force Fairford (Gloucestershire, England, Air Base)
item.media:credit: Pool photo by Frank Augstein
item.media:description: Prime Minister Keir Starmer has been mocked by President Trump for refusing to join the U.S.-Israeli war against Iran.

item.title: Iranians Voice Shock and Defiance in Face of Trump’s Looming Deadline
item.description: President Trump has threatened devastating attacks if Iran does not reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Some Iranians questioned what had happened to American values.
item.dc:creator: Erika Solomon and Sanam Mahoozi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: A destroyed building at Sharif University in Tehran on Tuesday.

item.title: Amid Escalating Threats, U.S. and Iran Float Ideas to End War
item.description: Both countries have offered plans that could form the basis of negotiations. So far, each side’s leaders have publicly dismissed the other side’s proposals.
item.dc:creator: Lynsey Chutel
item.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump during a news conference at the White House on Monday.

item.title: Gunman Killed in Shootout Near Israeli Consulate in Istanbul, Officials Say
item.description: Two other armed men and two police officers were wounded in the clash, the provincial governor said.
item.dc:creator: Ben Hubbard and Safak Timur
item.media:credit: Murad Sezer/Reuters
item.media:description: Police officers responding after gunfire was heard near the Israeli Consulate in Istanbul on Tuesday.

item.title: Weakened and Reluctant, Yemen’s Houthis Belatedly Enter War
item.description: Analysts say their delay in supporting Iran is partly because their capabilities were severely degraded by the U.S.-Israeli campaign last year.
item.dc:creator: Ismaeel Naar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Khaled Abdullah/Reuters
item.media:description: Houthi followers and Houthi-affiliated military police take part in a pro-Iran demonstration, as the U.S.-Israel conflict with Iran continues, in Sanaa, Yemen, on Monday.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Tyler Pager, Farnaz Fassihi, David E. Sanger and Eric Nagourney
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Hegseth Likens Easter Rescue of U.S. Airman to Resurrection of Jesus Christ
item.description: President Trump also asserted that God supports the American war against Iran “because God is good, and God wants to see people taken care of.”
item.dc:creator: Chris Cameron
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times
item.media:description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth speaking at a news conference at the White House on Monday.

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the War in Iran and the Middle East on Monday
item.description: President Trump escalated his threats ahead of his pending Tuesday deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Avishag Shaar-Yashuv for The New York Times
item.media:description: Inspecting damages on Monday inside a library of an ultra-Orthodox girls’ school following a missile strike in Tel Aviv.

item.title: Trump Says Iran Proposal Isn’t Enough to Stop Attacks on Bridges and Power Plants
item.description: President Trump has told Iran it must open the Strait of Hormuz by 8 p.m. Tuesday or face the consequences, although he has delayed previous deadlines.
item.dc:creator: Tyler Pager and Erika Solomon
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Damaged buildings in Tehran. President Trump said on Monday that the United States would consider helping to rebuild Iran if they can strike a deal.

item.title: 6 Takeaways From Trump’s News Conference on Iran
item.description: President Trump described the risky mission to rescue an Air Force colonel whose fighter jet had been shot down, but he offered no clear path out of the war.
item.dc:creator: Anton Troianovski and Luke Broadwater
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times
item.media:description: “We have to have a deal that’s acceptable to me,” President Trump said on Monday. “And part of that deal is going to be we want free traffic of oil and everything else.”

item.title: Iran’s 10-Point Proposal Demands an End to Attacks and Sanctions
item.description: As President Trump’s deadline for new attacks loomed, Iran conveyed its conditions through Pakistani intermediaries.
item.dc:creator: Max Bearak, Farnaz Fassihi, Shirin Hakim and Erika Solomon
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: A billboard in Tehran shows Iranian military commanders with American aircraft in a net.

item.title: As Trump Deadline Looms, Iran and U.S. Mix Threats With Hints of Deal
item.description: One potential off-ramp appeared when Iran offered a 10-point counterproposal for ending the war that President Trump called a significant step, if “not good enough.”
item.dc:creator: Zolan Kanno-Youngs, Erika Solomon, Aaron Boxerman and Michael Levenson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: A portrait of Iran’s late supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, in Tehran on Monday.

item.title: Israel Steps Up Attacks on Lebanon, Killing Dozens
item.description: Sunday was a particularly deadly day, as Israel targeted areas around the country — including a Christian area long regarded as safe by residents.
item.dc:creator: Amelia Nierenberg and Sarah Chaayto
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: David Guttenfelder/The New York Times
item.media:description: A neighborhood in Beirut on Monday, after an Israeli airstrike on Sunday.

item.title: Mapping the scale of the conflict.
item.dc:creator: Martín González Gómez

item.title: Iranians Condemn Strike on a Top University
item.description: Government officials and anti-government activists alike denounced the attacks on the Sharif University of Technology in Tehran, the latest Iranian center for higher education to be targeted.
item.dc:creator: Erika Solomon and Sanam Mahoozi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Francisco Seco/Associated Press
item.media:description: Workers clear debris on Monday at Sharif University of Technology after it was struck overnight.

item.title: Trump’s Board of Peace Gives Hamas Disarmament Deadline
item.description: The demand reflects both the U.S. administration’s eagerness to secure a lasting cease-fire in Gaza and its growing impatience with the Palestinian militant group.
item.dc:creator: Adam Rasgon, Natan Odenheimer and Abu Bakr Bashir
item.media:credit: Saher Alghorra for The New York Times
item.media:description: Armed members of Hamas in Khan Younis, Gaza, last year.

item.title: Key islands give Iran sway over the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: Josh Holder and Adina Renner

item.title: Trump Moves Deadline for Iran to Open Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The new deadline comes as the president and Iranian leaders have ramped up bombastic threats against one another.
item.dc:creator: Zolan Kanno-Youngs
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump has changed deadlines during the course of the Iran war.

item.title: At Least 4 Killed in Iranian Strike in Haifa, Israeli Officials Say
item.description: Rescue workers recovered four bodies from a residential building in the port city of Haifa after air defenses failed to intercept an Iranian strike, Israeli officials said.
item.dc:creator: Isabel Kershner
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Avishag Shaar-Yashuv for The New York Times
item.media:description: Emergency workers searching for missing people in the rubble of a residential building struck by a missile in Haifa, Israel, on Sunday.

item.title: The Latest Blows to Iran’s Leadership
item.description: An Iranian intelligence chief killed overnight on Monday was one of several Iranian officials who occupied their posts for only a few months.
item.dc:creator: Samuel Granados and Amelia Nierenberg
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Seyed Majid Khademi, Iranian Intelligence Chief, Killed in Overnight Attack
item.description: Israel claimed responsibility for the death of Major General Seyed Majid Khademi, the spy chief of the Islamic Revolutionary Guards Corps, the latest senior official to be killed in the war.
item.dc:creator: Amelia Nierenberg
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Office of the Iranian Supreme Leader, via Associated Press
item.media:description: A handout photograph released by the Office of the Iranian Supreme Leader showing Major General Seyed Majid Khademi, who was killed on Monday.

item.title: On Iran, Trump Keeps World Off Balance With Ever-Changing Threats
item.description: Global leaders are struggling in their efforts to find a way to end the American-Israeli war on Iran, and they are spooked about what President Trump might do next.
item.dc:creator: Michael D. Shear
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump has threatened to bomb crucial civilian infrastructure in Iran if Tehran does not agree to his demands by Tuesday.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Zolan Kanno-Youngs, Erika Solomon and Richard Pérez-Peña

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the War in the Middle East on Sunday
item.description: President Trump taunted Iran on social media, while strikes continued in Iran, Israel, Lebanon and some Gulf states.
item.dc:creator: Eve Sampson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Avishag Shaar-Yashuv for The New York Times
item.media:description: Emergency medical workers search through the rubble of a residential building struck by an Iranian missile attack on Sunday in Haifa, Israel.
